title: silene gallica var. lusitanica
intro: Silene gallica var. lusitanica, also known as the Portuguese catchfly, is a variety of the white campion plant, a flowering plant in the carnation family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Silene gallica var. lusitanica include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and tinctures that are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
